game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Root:** Root is a digital adaptation of an asymmetric board game where 2-4 players compete for control of a woodland map divided into clearings, each representing one of four unique factions (Marquise de Cat, Woodland Alliance, Eyrie Dynasties, Vagabond). Players interact by tapping clearings to select units, dragging units to move between adjacent clearings, and tapping cards in their hand to play them for actions or crafting, with all actions governed by faction-specific rules and a shared deck of cards. Each faction possesses distinct mechanics; for example, the Marquise de Cat focuses on building workshops and sawmills to generate victory points, while the Woodland Alliance uses sympathy tokens to incite rebellion and recruit warriors. Combat is resolved through dice rolls (e.g., 2 custom dice showing hits/misses) when units from opposing factions occupy the same clearing, with the attacker dealing damage first. The game concludes immediately when any player reaches 30 victory points or fulfills a specific faction-based victory condition, such as the Eyrie Dynasties completing their decree. There is no meta-progression between games; each session is a self-contained strategic challenge against AI opponents with adjustable difficulty levels or other human players, focusing on mastering the unique rules and interactions of each faction.
game_info.description: Command your woodland faction to victory! Control territories, build structures, and manage resources across a strategic map. Each faction has unique abilities: Marquise builds workshops for victory points, Alliance spreads sympathy to recruit warriors, Eyrie follows decrees for power, and Vagabond travels light collecting items. Reach 30 victory points first to win through clever positioning, faction-specific strategies, and tactical combat.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Navigate menu selections and move units between adjacent clearings
Space: Confirm selection, execute actions, and resolve combat
Z: Cancel current selection or action
Shift: Toggle between available actions for selected unit

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic game mechanics including unit selection, movement between clearings, and simple interactions. Validates that the game responds correctly to player inputs and state transitions work properly.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Execute a series of basic actions: select units, move between adjacent clearings, and interact with game objects. Test navigation through menus and confirm that game state updates appropriately.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The test successfully completes basic actions without errors. Units move correctly, selections work as intended, and game state remains consistent throughout the test sequence.

TEST_1.expected: The test successfully completes basic actions without errors. Units move correctly, selections work as intended, and game state remains consistent throughout the test sequence.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing the optimal win strategy by playing as Marquise de Cat faction. Focuses on building structures efficiently, controlling key clearings, and maximizing victory point generation to reach 30 points.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Prioritize building workshops and sawmills in high-value clearings. Maintain military presence to protect structures. Efficiently use cards for crafting and actions. Balance expansion with defensive positioning to accumulate victory points steadily.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The test achieves GAME_OVER_WIN state by reaching 30 victory points. Validates that victory conditions work correctly and that the Marquise faction can win through structure-based point generation.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing combat mechanics and multi-faction interactions. Validates that combat resolution works correctly with proper damage calculation and that different factions can engage in meaningful conflict.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Deliberately move units into contested clearings to trigger combat. Test different combat scenarios with varying numbers of units. Verify that combat outcomes affect game state appropriately.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Combat resolves correctly with proper damage application. Unit counts update accurately after battles. Game state remains consistent after multiple combat encounters.
